name: Emergency Food Kits
description: 7-day dry ration kits containing rice, dal, salt, cooking oil, and biscuits. Packaged for distribution to cyclone-affected families.

name: Emergency Food Kits
description: 7-day dry ration kits for northern Odisha district distribution. Pre-positioned ahead of cyclone season.

name: Potable Water Tankers
description: 10,000-litre water tankers for deployment to areas with contaminated or cut groundwater supply post-cyclone.

name: Portable Water Purification Units
description: Reverse osmosis water purification units capable of processing 5,000 litres/hour. Deployed to shelters and medical facilities.

name: Emergency Medical Kits
description: WHO-standard emergency medical kits covering wound care, ORS, antibiotics, and trauma supplies. Each kit serves 10 patients for one week.

name: Emergency Medical Kits
description: Emergency medical kits pre-positioned for northern Odisha districts. Stock is limited after last quarter's flood response draw-down.

name: Surgical Supply Kits
description: Sterile surgical supply kits for mass casualty event response. Maintained at AIIMS Bhubaneswar for surge capacity activation.

name: Emergency Relief Trucks
description: 10-tonne capacity flat-bed and enclosed trucks for relief supply movement. Maintained by state civil defence and private requisition pool.

name: NDRF Response Vehicles
description: Specialised NDRF fleet including 4WD command vehicles, communication vans, and light rescue trucks. Ready for rapid deployment within 2 hours of activation.

name: Inflatable Rescue Boats
description: Motorised inflatable rescue boats for flood and storm surge rescue operations. Currently deployed to Kendrapara and Puri coastal areas.

name: Emergency Helicopters
description: State government and Air Force helicopters tasked for disaster relief. Capable of aerial supply drops, medical evacuation, and search-and-rescue over flooded terrain.

name: Fuel Reserves (Diesel / Aviation)
description: IOCL strategic fuel reserves at Paradip. Supplies diesel for emergency vehicles, generators, and aviation fuel for relief helicopters. At high risk during cyclone landfall.

name: Fuel Reserves (Diesel)
description: Secondary diesel reserve at Cuttack logistics hub for emergency vehicle fuelling during road relief operations.

name: Portable Generators
description: 15–30 kVA diesel generators for hospitals, shelters, and command centers during grid power outages. Essential for hospital operations post-landfall.

name: Field Portable Generators
description: Compact field generators for NDRF rescue camp operations, field hospitals, and temporary command posts in disaster zones.

name: Satellite Communication Units (VSAT)
description: VSAT satellite communication terminals for deployment to field command posts when terrestrial telecom networks fail during cyclone. ISRO SATCOM bandwidth allocated.

name: HF/VHF Radio Sets
description: High-frequency and VHF handheld radio sets for field team communication. Primary fallback when mobile networks are down.

name: Temporary Shelter Kits
description: Waterproof tarpaulin and pole shelter kits for families whose homes are destroyed or inaccessible. Each kit shelters one family of 5 for 4 weeks.

name: NDRF Rescue Teams
description: Each NDRF team consists of 45 trained rescuers with specialised equipment for flood, building collapse, and water rescue. Teams currently deployed to high-risk coastal districts.

name: Ambulances
description: Advanced life support ambulances at AIIMS Bhubaneswar for mass casualty transport from field triage points to tertiary hospital.

name: Ambulances
description: SCB Medical College ambulance fleet for casualty transport across northern Odisha districts.
